title: Evaluator-optimizer review gate before ticket close
source: Search description: Anthropic 'Building Effective Agents' evaluator-optimizer workflow + LLM-as-judge review loops (live fetch blocked)
description: Tickets currently move to done after the worker self-reports completion. An automated evaluator pass (separate agent grading the diff against the ticket's acceptance criteria, looping back with feedback once or twice) catches incomplete or off-spec work before it lands, mirroring the evaluator-optimizer pattern that materially raises output quality for code tasks.
proposed_changes: Add an `EvalResult` schema (verdict: pass/revise, blocking_issues[], score). In workers.py after a worker finishes, spawn an evaluator `claude -p` agent given the ticket spec + git diff; if verdict is 'revise', re-delegate to the same worker with blocking_issues injected, capped at 2 iterations. Only transition the ticket to done on pass (or after cap, flag for human review). Wire the loop count into orchestrate.py's sprint cycle and emit roro 'eval_pass'/'eval_revise' events.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve timed out after 300s
